node.statement: (Remark: Slack variable formulation) Introducing slack variables S_i = Φ_i(x) - a_i ∈ W_i^+ rewrites conic inequalities as equalities over the product cone V^+ × Π_i W_i^+. The face of the product cone at (x, (S_i)) has dimension d_F(x) + Σ_i d_F(S_i in W_i^+), and BP gives d_F(x) + Σ_i d_F(S_i) ≤ Σ_i dim(W_i). This is at least as tight as the conic inequality bound when some slack has non-trivial face. However, face-reduction algorithms cannot control whether they reduce rank(X) or kill slacks.
